evals.name: sales-data-exploration
evals.prompt: I just got this sales dataset from the finance team. It's at eda-workspace/test-datasets/sales_data.csv — can you take a look and tell me what's going on with our sales? We've got about 500 rows of daily transactions. I need to understand the patterns before our quarterly meeting next week.
evals.expected_output: Comprehensive EDA with: data overview, distribution analysis of revenue/units, correlation matrix, regional comparisons, time-series trends, and actionable summary. Should produce visualizations and a structured report.

assertions.text: Includes data shape, column types, and basic statistics
Identifies correlation between units_sold, unit_price, discount_pct, and revenue
Produces at least 3 different visualization types (histogram, heatmap, bar chart, etc.)
Analyzes regional differences in sales patterns
Provides actionable insights or recommendations, not just raw stats
Saves structured EDA report to .claude/eda_report.json

evals.name: messy-data-quality
evals.prompt: Hey, there's something off with our customer database export at eda-workspace/test-datasets/messy_customer_data.csv. I've been hearing complaints about data quality. Can you investigate what's wrong? I need to know what we need to fix before we can use this for any analytics.
evals.expected_output: Should catch: impossible ages, duplicate rows, inconsistent city/status labels, future dates, constant column, near-perfect correlation (leakage signal), suspiciously round incomes, missing values, and extreme outlier in score.

assertions.text: Identifies impossible/invalid ages (negative, >130)
Detects duplicate rows in the dataset
Catches inconsistent categorical labels in city or status columns (case variations)
Flags future dates (2030) in signup_date
Identifies 'country' as a constant/zero-variance column
Detects near-perfect correlation between score and score_copy (potential leakage)
Reports missing values in income column
Flags the extreme outlier (99999) in score column

evals.name: classification-target-analysis
evals.prompt: We're building a churn prediction model and I have our historical data at eda-workspace/test-datasets/churn_data.csv. 600 customers with features like tenure, charges, support tickets, etc. The target is 'churned'. Can you do a thorough analysis? I want to know which features matter most and if there are any data issues before we start modeling.
evals.expected_output: Should include: class imbalance analysis of churned column, feature-target relationships, correlation analysis flagging total_charges/tenure relationship, missing value report, feature importance indicators, and modeling recommendations.

assertions.text: Analyzes class distribution/imbalance of the churned target variable
Examines relationships between features and the churn target
Identifies that total_charges = tenure * monthly_charges (derived/redundant feature)
Reports missing values in total_charges and payment_method
Provides pre-modeling recommendations (handle imbalance, address missing data, feature engineering ideas)
Creates at least 2 visualizations showing feature-target relationships
